But I want to change the right block, not the center block. So maybe I need to click on a separator to the right of the right block, but there is no such separator (the right block is adjacent to the charcoal frame).

So clicking at (36, 44) changed the CENTER block from blue to red, not the left block! The separator at cols 44-45 is between the left and center blocks, and clicking on it changed the center block (to the right of the separator).
So the rule is: clicking on a separator toggles the block to the RIGHT of the separator.

None of the unframed grids have a top row of blue, red, blue with the center block red. Wait, the top-left and top-right grids have top rows of blue, red, blue. But the center block is red, not the center block being red.

Now let me check the middle rows:

- Top-right middle row: red, center, red (left=red, center=center, right=red)

But what if I click on the separator at cols 52-53? That should change the right block (cols 54-59).
